content:
🚀 tiptop | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Monitoring system resource usage and performance from the command line.
✨ Key Features: • Command-line system monitoring • Real-time system statistics • Graphs system data • Cross-platform compatibility • Textual-based interface
📖 Summary: Tiptop is a command-line system monitoring tool written in Python, inspired by 'top'. It provides real-time system statistics and visualizes them using graphs, working across all operating systems. It leverages Textual for its interface and psutil for data retrieval.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ SAM3-UNet | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Adapting large foundation models like SAM3 for specific image segmentation tasks with minimal computational resources.
✨ Key Features: • Simplified adaptation of SAM3 • Parameter-efficient fine-tuning • Lightweight U-Net-style decoder • Low GPU memory usage (< 6GB) • Outperforms prior methods on tasks like mirror and salient object detection
📖 Summary: SAM3-UNet is a simplified adaptation of the Segment Anything Model 3 (SAM3) designed for efficient fine-tuning on downstream tasks. It combines a SAM3 image encoder with a parameter-efficient adapter and a lightweight U-Net decoder, achieving competitive performance with significantly reduced GPU memory requirements.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 oc-claw | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a visually engaging and informative desktop companion that keeps users updated on the status and activity of their AI coding assistants.
✨ Key Features: • Real-time monitoring of AI coding agent activity • Desktop pet character with animations based on agent status • Auto-discovery of local OpenClaw agents • Live session viewing for Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor
📖 Summary: OC-Claw is a desktop application that acts as a virtual pet to monitor the real-time activity of AI coding agents like OpenClaw, Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor. It provides visual feedback through character animations and offers detailed session information, chat history, and usage metrics.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 datatui | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Exploring and manipulating data directly within the terminal environment using a keyboard-first interface.
✨ Key Features: • Tabbed data views with quick navigation • CSV/TSV, Excel, and SQLite import • Polars-backed SQL queries and lazy evaluation • Sorting, filtering, and column management • Find, Find All, and value viewer
📖 Summary: DataTUI is a fast, keyboard-first terminal UI application built with Rust and Ratatui for exploring various data formats. It supports CSV/TSV, Excel, and SQLite, offering features like tabbed views, sorting, filtering, and SQL querying via Polars.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 BlogBowl | Ruby
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling users to easily create and manage blogs, changelogs, and help documentation without requiring any coding knowledge.
✨ Key Features: • Launch a full-featured blog or help center in minutes • Prebuilt SEO-optimized and lightning-fast templates • Notion-like editor • Built-in newsletter support with Postmark integration • Custom domain support
📖 Summary: BlogBowl is an open-source, self-hosted blogging platform that allows users to quickly launch SEO-optimized blogs, product changelogs, and help documentation without coding. It features a Notion-like editor, built-in newsletter support with Postmark integration, and custom domain capabilities.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 squirreldisk | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Identifying and visualizing large files and directories to free up disk space.
✨ Key Features: • Fast scan and deep directory scanning • Disk scanning or pick a directory • External disks real-time detection • Sunburst chart for disk usage visualization • Drag and drop for collecting items to delete
📖 Summary: SquirrelDisk is a cross-platform desktop application built with Rust and React (Tauri) for analyzing disk usage. It helps users quickly identify large files and folders consuming hard drive space, offering a visual representation of disk utilization through a sunburst chart.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
